LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT UPS'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE DISCRIMINATION CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT ALSO GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM. THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE AND FAILURE TO ENGAGE CAUSES OF ACTION. FURTHER THE COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO USE OF THE "RELEASE OF CLAIMS". PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING ON AUGUST 9, 2011. PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER SEE WORD MINUTES FOR DETAILS.

GIORGI; NOT REPORTED. - AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL MUST REMIT $1000 IN SANCTIONS AND RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL BY SEPTEMBER 9, 2011. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. DEFENDANT IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LENARD RITZMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 --- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION ON THE HARASSMENT CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE "BLACK TWINKIE" COMMENT OCCURRED ON DECEMBER 5, 2007. IF IT DID, THEN PLAINTIFF CAN RELY ON THE CONTINUING VIOLATION DOCTRINE AND DEFENDANT'S PRIOR ON-GOING CONDUCT IS SUFFICIENT TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE COMMENTS WERE ISOLATED AND SPORADIC AND CONSTITUTED HARASSMENT. FURTHER, THE COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO USE OF THE "RELEASE OF CLAIMS". FIRST, THE COURT IS NOT USING IT FOR THE PURPOSE OF DETERMINING LIABILITY. THIS EVIDENCE MERELY RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE FAILURE TO ENGAGE CAUSE OF ACTION. MOREOVER, DEFENDANT ADMITS THAT THE RELEASE WAS RELATED TO A COMPLETELY SEPARATE INVESTIGATION OF PLAINTIFF'S OTHER WORKPLACE ISSUES. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING ON AUGUST 9, 2011. PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-04-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Notice Of Motion To 1) Compel Responses To Interrogatories; 2) To Compel Further Respones To Production Of Documents, And 3) For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC'S MOTION TO 1) COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES; 2) TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND 3) FOR SANCTIONS.
Appearances: Christina F. Latta of Paul Hastings (856.7281) for defendant and moving party; Robert Bone (707.525.8999) and Stephen R. Jaffe (618.0100) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (Note: this matter was heard on the Law and Motion calendar). (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported.
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: defendants' motion to compel response to interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff's counsel must remit $1000 in sanctions and responses to defendant's counsel by September 9, 2011. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Defendant is directed to submit a proposed form of order.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-20-2011 Continued To Sep-26-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2011 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LENARD RITZMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Stephen R. Jaffe of the Jaffe Law Firm (618.0100) and Robert M. Bone of the Law Office of Robert M. Bone (707.525.8999) for plaintiff and responding party; Katherine C. Huibonhoa of Paul Hastings (856.7035) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
---
After hearing, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the Court denies Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication on the harassment cause of action. There is a triable issue of fact as to whether the "black Twinkie" comment occurred on December 5, 2007. If it did, then Plaintiff can rely on the continuing violation doctrine and Defendant's prior on-going conduct is sufficient to create a triable issue of fact as to whether the comments were isolated and sporadic and constituted harassment. Further, the court overrules defendant's objection to use of the "release of claims". First, the court is not using it for the purpose of determining liability. This evidence merely raises a triable issue of fact as to the failure to engage cause of action. Moreover, defendant admits that the release was related to a completely separate investigation of plaintiff's other workplace issues. Parties notified of the court's ruling on August 9, 2011. Plaintiff is directed to submit a proposed form of order.
